rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type annexes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Insert AGREE annex blocks within `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax in the appropriate AADL scope.
generic_rules: Use visible component ports and features in AGREE contracts; avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in type-level annexes.
Ensure AGREE variable types match AADL data types, especially for boolean, integer, and real comparisons.
Do not include AADL property names (e.g., Deadline, Period) as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
Write range assumptions and output guarantees using visible feature names and concrete bounds from the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible identifiers from the AADL component scope.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must align with the actual AADL data types of the ports or variables involved.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes or using subcomponent ports in type-level contracts.
Do not encode AADL timing or resource properties (e.g., ms, Hz) as AGREE expressions unless they are explicitly typed in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of component structure
